facts.text: Atlanta was renamed in 1845 to honor the Western and Atlantic Railroad, a name suggested by engineer John Edgar Thomson.
The city’s world‑famous airport, Hartsfield‑Jackson Atlanta International, became the busiest in the world by passenger traffic in 1998 and remains a Delta hub.
Atlanta hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics, the first U.S. city to win the bid and the largest U.S. Olympic host to date.
The Martin Luther King Jr. National Historical Park preserves the civil‑rights leader’s childhood home and the historic Ebenezer Baptist Church where he preached.
Coca‑Cola’s corporate headquarters sits in Atlanta, and the World of Coca‑Cola museum chronicles the brand’s global impact.
